name: Mike Wofford
abstract: Michael Wofford (February 25, 1938 – September 19, 2025) was an American jazz pianist and composer. He was an accompanist to singers Sarah Vaughan (in 1979) and Ella Fitzgerald (1989–1994).

interviews.title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald, Singer
JVC Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald, Singer

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Barbutti
about_quotes.evidence: So I got Mike Wofford on piano... So we had three horns, three rhythm, and a girl singer... And we were together like three months
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Barbutti interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 8, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: Mike Wofford, whom I used to go out and listen to a lot in San Diego, he was a big influence on my playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: he got myself and Mike Wofford and Brian Bromberg and he just stood up and sang on this album... we went out and did a few dates like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Holly Hofmann
about_quotes.evidence: I have the phenomenal rhythm section here of Mike Wofford... Holly employs pianist Mike Wofford in her San Diego ensemble.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holly Hofmann interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Magnusson
about_quotes.evidence: It’s Mike Wofford playing piano and Joe LaBarbara, myself and Bud. And we did all music associated with Bill Evans.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Magnusson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Mike Wofford. >> [INTERVIEWEE] Mike Wofford. There’s a lot of good musicians here.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998
